personal.name: Joakim Moléni
personal.title: Systems Engineer — Core Banking, IBM z/OS

variants.title: Core Systems
variants.description: Prioritises mainframe, reliability and transaction-heavy work.

variants.title: Modern Backend
variants.description: Highlights backend, integrations and modern service delivery.

variants.title: Platform & Delivery
variants.description: Balances architecture, ownership and delivery across systems.

profile: Systems engineer at the deepest layer of core banking — writing assembler and COBOL on IBM z/OS where a single defect can take down the bank. One of roughly seven engineers in Sweden working at this level. My range spans from bare-metal mainframe code to modern web frontends and internal developer tooling. Shaped by a lifetime of building — from custom hardware at age five to leading in business — I bring both technical depth and operational discipline to everything I ship.

highlights: Write and maintain assembler and COBOL at the core of SEB’s banking platform — code where a single defect can halt the bank.
Part of a five-person team recognised as among the strongest mainframe developers at SEB, routinely solving problems others can’t.
Review COBOL across teams, catch missed edge cases, and drive quality in production-critical transaction paths.
Lead risk analysis and incident investigation for core ledger systems. Build internal documentation tooling that replaced scattered Word files and old binders.
Pushing to production within the first week of COBOL training — the fastest in the cohort.
Built and maintained transaction logic for deposits and payments on core banking systems.
Worked across system boundaries in Linux and distributed environments under real production constraints.
Designed and shipped Node.js APIs powering a real-time analytics product for competitive gaming.
Built AWS deployment pipelines and owned the full delivery path from code to production.
Owned integration points between data pipelines and customer-facing services under changing load.
Built service-backed applications with SQL data models and integration-heavy workflows.
Maintained Linux-hosted solutions with focus on production durability.
14 years of leading teams, owning outcomes and building operational discipline — skills that now shape how I approach system design.
Consistently operated in environments where uptime, accountability and execution quality were non-negotiable.
Track record among the top sellers and business developers nationally before switching to engineering.

projects.name: Documentation Hub for Mainframe Teams
projects.description: A web-based internal tool replacing scattered Word docs, old HTML pages and physical binders with a searchable, structured documentation platform built for mainframe developers and their managers.
